MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY (FACT DISCOVERY)(ADDED PER STAFF ATTY 12/27/22)
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. Jeffrey Windsor, Esq. of WINDSOR PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION (Tel: 650-638-2360) for Plaintiff;
Atty. Kelli George, Esq. of JEANETTE N. LITTLE & ASSOCIATES Employees of the Law Department State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company (Tel: 925-225-6838) for the Defendant.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY (FACT DISCOVERY).

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted.

The Court vacates its tentative ruling, and rules as follows: Plaintiff's motion to reopen discovery is granted solely to give the Plaintiff and Defendant the opportunity to take the deposition of one witness (whose name is to be identified from the police report). Any failure to locate or take the deposition of this witness will not be the basis for a motion to continue the current trial date.

Counsel for plaintiff/moving party is to prepare a proposed order in Word format, submit it to counsel for defendant for approval as to form, and then submit it to the pro tem judge at rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane / R. Sahatjian; Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR No. 13714, Email: apourtabib@gmail.com, Tel: 415-601-7054 (302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-04-2022 Continued To Apr-17-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jeffrey Windsor, Esq. (650.638.2360; jeff@windsor-plc.com) of Windsor Professional Law Corporation for Plaintiff.
Kelli George, Esq. (925.225.6838; kelli.george@statefarm.com) of Jeanette N. Little & Associates for Defendant.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CATHERINE HANIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff's motion to amend is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff unduly delayed filing this motion and allowing amendment will prejudice defendant. The court may deny amendment based on an unwarranted delay and prejudice to the defendant. (See Rainer v. Buena Cmty. Mem'l Hosp. (1971) 18 Cal.App.3d 240, 257-259.) Indeed, an unexplained delay alone is grounds to deny amendment. (See Record v. Reason (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 472, 486 ["even if a good amendment is proposed in proper form, unwarranted delay in presenting it may-of itself-be a valid reason for denial."].)

Here, plaintiff does not convincingly argue that recent discovery compels the amendment. First, plaintiff could have pleaded these claims at the inception of the case since she witnessed defendant's allegedly assaultive conduct when it occurred. Second, while defendant admitted at deposition that he slammed on his brakes in front of plaintiff to get away from her, that deposition occurred on March 16, 2022, and plaintiff filed this motion on the eve of trial on October 18, 2022. (The trial date was December 12, 2022, when plaintiff filed this motion). Third, the police report-which was produced years ago-also showed the alleged assault. One of the witnesses on the scene noted that defendant was trying to run plaintiff off the road.

If the court were to allow amendment at this point, defendant would be prejudiced since he has no time to conduct discovery or bring a dispositive motion attacking the new claims, including the new prayer for punitive damages. While the court notes that the Presiding Judge recently continued the trial date to April 17, 2023, only expert discovery tracks the new trial date. Therefore, defendant would be unable to conduct general discovery regarding the new claims. It would also not be feasible for defendant to file a motion for summary adjudication, which requires at least 75 days notice.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
